The Rubus genus consists of a group of thorny shrubs that produce edible fruits, collectively known as bramble fruits. The health benefits of Rubus plants include their high antioxidant content, which may help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. Bioactive constituents of Rubus plants include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which are responsible for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Rubus plants are commonly used to make jams, jellies, teas, and supplements, with blackberry and raspberry being among the most popular herbal preparations.
